---
title: "qc-atelier — Technical Documentation and Decision Log"
date: "2026-04-19"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: false
---

*Purpose: Authoritative context document for all development conversations about qc-atelier. Feed this document in full at the start of any new session.*

---

## What is qc-atelier

qc-atelier is a personal practitioner toolkit built on top of the `qc` CLI (`qualitative-coding` on PyPI). It is a single researcher's tool, connected to a workshop on citational practices in Viking Age archaeology ("Citational Politics and Practices in Viking Age Archaeology and Beyond", University of Oslo, in collaboration with Julie Lund and Isak Roalkvam, seeded from Lund & Sindbæk 2022 "Crossing the Maelstrom").

The toolkit is the primary active technical focus of this researcher's post-doctoral work. The code lives in `CITF-Postdoc` on GitHub (public). Data lives in `qc/`, `private/`, and `data/` as git submodules on a university-managed GitLab instance (private).

**The four tools:**

| Tool | Role | Status |
|---|---|---|
| qc-scheme | Passive — documents codes, manages snapshots and history | Active |
| qc-refactor | Active — structural operations on the code system | Active |
| qc-viz | Passive — visual corpus reader | Active |
| qc-reflect | Active — LLM-assisted reflexive analysis | Deprioritised |

**The key distinction:** qc-scheme and qc-viz are passive/responsive — they read and display. qc-refactor is active — it executes structural changes. This separation is intentional and must be maintained.

**Technical environment:** Python 3.14 on macOS ARM, `.venv` virtual environment, GROBID 0.8.1 via Docker, Ollama with `qwen3:35b` (thinking mode disabled via `"think": false`). qc installed globally via pipx at `/Users/zackbatist/.local/bin/qc`.

---

## The qc CLI — upstream dependency

`qc` stores code systems as bare YAML trees and coding applications in a SQLite database.

**Core data structures:**

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| `codebook.yaml` | Code system as bare YAML tree (names + hierarchy only) |
| `qualitative_coding.sqlite3` | SQLite database — live source of truth for all code applications |
| `settings.yaml` | qc project configuration |
| `qualitative_coding.log` | Structured operation log written by qc |

**qc-atelier exports** (generated by pre-render script, not by qc itself):

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| `qc/json/*.json` | Per-document coding exports: `[{code, document, line, text}]` |
| `qc/corpus/*.txt` | Source interview transcripts |

**Critical:** `qc/json/*.json` are render-time exports by qc-atelier, not qc's live data. `qualitative_coding.sqlite3` is qc's live source of truth. qc-atelier must never write to the SQLite database directly.

**qc data model** (verified from source — `corpus.py`, `models.py`):

The database uses SQLAlchemy over SQLite. Key tables: `Code` (name as primary key), `Coder`, `Document`, `DocumentIndex`, `Location` (line range), `CodedLine` (core coding record: code + coder + line).

`qc codes rename` reassigns `CodedLine.code_id` in the database then calls `update_codebook()` to sync `codebook.yaml`. This is also how merge works — multiple old codes renamed to one new code. `codebook.yaml` is therefore derived from the database, not the source of truth.

**What qc does NOT have:**

- `qc codes delete` — no command exists to remove a code and its corpus applications
- `qc codes move` — no command exists to reassign a code's parent in the hierarchy

---

## Tools

### qc-scheme

**Role:** Passive. The primary documentation and review interface. Does not modify code structure.

qc-scheme enriches the bare YAML code system with what `qc` cannot store: scope definitions, rationale, usage notes, provenance, status, and canonical corpus examples, kept in `codebook.json` alongside an append-only edit history. It makes the evolution of the code system deliberate and auditable.

**What it does:**

- GUI editor for per-code documentation: scope, rationale, usage_notes, provenance, status, examples
- Per-code and document-level history (field edits + refactor events from qc-refactor, merged and sorted)
- Snapshot system: named checkpoints and forks in `qc/snapshots/`
- Cross-snapshot diffing (Fields and JSON views)
- Multi-code comparison (table and cards views, bulk status changes)
- Export: YAML, MD, QMD, HTML, CSV, PDF

**Two-phase operation:**

```
RENDER TIME                              RUNTIME
───────────                              ───────
quarto render qc-scheme.qmd             browser loads qc/qc-scheme.html
     │                                        │
     ▼                                        ▼
Lua filter reads:                        JS calls GET /docs/load
  codebook.yaml        ──bakes──►        loads codebook.json
  qc/json/*.json       into HTML              │
  qc-scheme.js                           user edits fields
  qc-scheme.css                               │
     │                                  scheduleSave() 800ms
     ▼                                        │
qc/qc-scheme.html                       POST /docs/save
                                              │
                                        codebook.json updated
```

| Change | Re-render needed? |
|---|---|
| Codes added/removed/renamed in `codebook.yaml` | Yes |
| Corpus `qc/json/` files updated | Yes (use counts stale) |
| JS or CSS source files changed | Yes |
| Documentation fields edited | No |
| Status changes | No |
| Snapshot or fork created | No |
| qc-refactor writes to `codebook.json` | No (read at runtime) |

**Documentation fields:**

| Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `scope` | What this code captures; how to apply it |
| `rationale` | Why this code exists; how it differs from siblings |
| `usage_notes` | Edge cases, exclusions, common confusions |
| `provenance` | Genealogy — created from, renamed from, merged with. Written by qc-refactor on execution. |
| `examples` | Pinned canonical corpus excerpts with annotations |
| `status` | active / experimental / deprecated |
| `_log` | Append-only per-field edit history (internal) |
| `_baseline` | Snapshot of field values at first load (internal) |

**`codebook.json` structure:**

```json
{
  "saved": "ISO timestamp",
  "codes": {
    "CodeName": {
      "scope": "...", "rationale": "...", "usage_notes": "...",
      "provenance": "...", "status": "active|experimental|deprecated|""",
      "examples": [{doc, line, note, text}],
      "_log": [{ts, field, from, to}],
      "_baseline": {scope, rationale, usage_notes, provenance, status}
    }
  },
  "tree": [...],
  "overrides": {},
  "changelog": [
    {"ts": "ISO", "type": "open|save|refactor|bulk-status", "detail": "..."}
  ]
}
```

**Application state (key fields):**

```javascript
state = {
  docs:          { codes: {...} },    // live codebook.json data
  selected:      null,                // currently selected code name
  multiSelected: Set,                 // multi-selected codes
  treeOverrides: {},                  // {codeName: parentName} — retained for rendering machinery
  excerpts:      {},                  // {codeName: [{doc, line, text}]} — lazy loaded
  snapshots:     [],
  theme:         'dark'|'light',
}
```

**UI reference:**

*Sidebar* — full indented code tree. Click to select; Shift/Cmd-click for multi-select. Pip icons for export selection. Status indicators (deprecated → dimmed, experimental → italic). Use counts per node.

*Documentation tab* — editable fields: Scope, Rationale, Usage notes, Provenance, Status. Autosaves on blur or 2.5s inactivity. Debounced 800ms server save.

*Examples tab* — corpus excerpts alongside pinned canonical examples. Pin/unpin controls.

*History tab* — per-code field edit timeline. Click to inspect; click two to diff.

*Multi-selection toolbar* — bulk status change, comparison table, cards view.

*Topbar* — mode switcher: Codebook / History / Snapshots.

**Snapshot system:**

Snapshots are named directories in `qc/snapshots/` containing `codebook.yaml` and `codebook.json` at the moment of creation. `lineage.json` maps each snapshot to its parent and note. `.working_parent` tracks the currently loaded snapshot.

```
No prior snapshot:
  snapshot → codebook_TIMESTAMP_label
Prior snapshot exists:
  snapshot → codebook_TIMESTAMP_label     (same chain)
  fork    → codebook-{segment}_TIMESTAMP_HASH4   (new chain)
```

**History and diffing:**

All per-code `_log` entries and all `changelog` events (including `refactor` events from qc-refactor) merged and sorted newest first. A/B colour coding: first clicked = teal, second = amber. Fields view: side-by-side before/after per changed field. JSON view: full serialised diff with accordion for long unchanged runs (>30 lines), no LCS size limit.

**Export system:**

All exports generated client-side. Scope controlled by pip selection in sidebar.

| Format | Description |
|---|---|
| YAML | Full YAML with all documentation fields |
| MD | Markdown, one section per code |
| QMD | Quarto document with YAML front matter |
| HTML | Standalone formatted HTML |
| CSV | One row per code, all fields as columns |
| PDF | Landscape table via jsPDF + jsPDF-autotable (CDN) |

**Globals injected by Lua filter:**
```javascript
CODEBOOK_TREE   // flat list of {name, parent, depth, prefix}
CORPUS_COUNTS   // {codeName: {total, docs}}
DOCS_DATA       // {codes: {}} — empty shell; live data loaded at runtime
DOCS_CONFIG     // {server_port, scheme_path, json_dir}
```

**Key files:**
```
qc-scheme.qmd
qc-atelier/qc-scheme/
  qc-scheme-filter.lua     ← pwd fallback for shared path resolution
  qc-scheme.js
  qc-scheme.css
qc/codebook.json           ← rich metadata sidecar (autosaved 800ms)
qc/.working_parent         ← ephemeral: last snapshot dir opened/created
```

**Known limitations:**

- Structural changes require re-render
- Snapshot with different tree structure: docs load correctly but tree display reflects baked structure
- `treeOverrides` retained in JS but UI controls removed — parent moves now through qc-refactor
- jsPDF from CDN — PDF export requires network

---

### qc-refactor

**Role:** Active. The only tool that modifies code structure.

**What it does:**

- Stage rename, merge, move, and deprecate operations in a queue
- Preview the post-operation code tree and affected corpus segments before committing
- Inspect the generated bash script
- Execute the queue with a required session note
- Create a named snapshot before execution
- Write provenance notes and changelog entries back to `codebook.json`

**Two-phase operation:**

```
RENDER TIME                              RUNTIME
───────────                              ───────
quarto render qc-refactor.qmd           browser loads qc/qc-refactor.html
     │                                        │
     ▼                                        ▼
Lua filter bakes:                        JS calls GET /docs/load
  codebook.yaml        ──bakes──►        loads codebook.json for inline doc display
  qc/json/*.json       into HTML              │
  qc-refactor.js                         user stages operations
  qc-refactor.css                        user writes session note
     │                                        │
     ▼                                   POST /refactor/execute
qc/qc-refactor.html                           │
                                    ┌─────────┴──────────┐
                                    ▼                    ▼
                               snapshot created    qc codes rename
                               codebook.json       (subprocess)
                               updated
```

**Operations:**

| Type | Mechanism | Corpus affected? |
|---|---|---|
| Rename | `qc codes rename A B` subprocess | Yes |
| Merge | `qc codes rename A B C` subprocess | Yes |
| Move | Direct `codebook.yaml` edit (server-side regex) | No |
| Deprecate | `status: "deprecated"` in `codebook.json` | No |
| Delete | Not implemented — no `qc codes delete` exists | — |

**Provenance written on execution:**

- Rename: `"Renamed from OldCode (TIMESTAMP)"`
- Merge: `"Merged from A, B (TIMESTAMP): session note"`
- Move: `"Moved to NewParent (TIMESTAMP)"`

**UI reference:**

*Left panel* — op form (Rename / Merge / Move / Deprecate tabs), per-type queue lists, session note textarea, execute button.

*Op form* — searchable hierarchical code picker with depth indentation and corpus counts. When a code is selected its existing docs are shown as editable textareas (edits held in `state.docsEdits`, written to `codebook.json` on execution). Merge: source docs read-only (they disappear), target pre-populated with attribution.

*Queue list* — read-only record of staged operations. Remove button per item.

*Session note* — persistent textarea. Required before execution. Shows live snapshot name preview as you type.

*Execute button* — disabled until operations staged and note written. No modal.

*Right panel — Preview (Tree diff / Corpus impact) / Script / Results / History.*

*Tree diff* — post-operation accordion tree. Affected branches expanded. Changes annotated: `(was OldName)`, `(+N merged: A, B)`, `(moved)`, `(deprecated)`.

*Corpus impact* — per-operation segment view, expandable excerpts. Move excluded (no corpus change).

*Script* — generated bash script, copyable.

*Results* — snapshot confirmation card, session note, per-operation ✓/✗ with stdout.

*History* — `refactor` entries from `codebook.json` changelog, newest first: timestamp, pass/fail, note, snapshot name, operations.

**Execution flow:**

1. Pre-execution snapshot — creates `codebook_YYYYMMDD-HHMM_label` in `qc/snapshots/`, copies `codebook.yaml` and `codebook.json`, updates `lineage.json` and `.working_parent`
2. Run operations — rename/merge via `qc codes rename` subprocess (`cwd=project_root`, `timeout=30s`); move via `POST /refactor/move`; deprecate via server setting status field
3. Update `codebook.json` — apply `docs_edits` with `_log` entries, update `provenance`, append `refactor` event to `changelog`
4. Return per-operation `{cmd, ok, output}` plus snapshot name

**Session note and versioning:**

The session note is required before execution. It becomes the snapshot label (sanitised, truncated to 30 chars), is written to the `refactor` changelog entry as `summary`, and seeds the `provenance` field of affected codes.

**Globals baked at render time:**
```javascript
CODEBOOK_TREE    // {name, parent, depth, prefix}[]
CORPUS_COUNTS    // {codeName: {total, docs}}
CORPUS_DATA      // {codeName: [{document, line, text}]} — capped 30/code
REFACTOR_CONFIG  // {server_port, scheme_path, json_dir}
```

**`/refactor/execute` payload:**
```json
{
  "operations": [{"id": 1, "type": "rename", "sources": ["OldCode"], "target": "NewCode"}],
  "summary": "session note text",
  "script": "#!/bin/bash
qc codes rename OldCode NewCode",
  "scheme_path": "/abs/path/to/qc/codebook.json",
  "docs_edits": {"CodeName": {"provenance": "updated text"}}
}
```

**Key files:**
```
qc-refactor.qmd                  ← filter under format: html: filters:, NOT top-level filters:
qc-atelier/qc-refactor/
  qc-refactor-filter.lua         ← skeleton HTML in [[...]] block; JS script at end of body
  qc-refactor.js
  qc-refactor.css
```

**Known limitations:**

- Re-render required after execution — `CODEBOOK_TREE`, `CORPUS_DATA`, `CORPUS_COUNTS` stale until then
- No delete operation — use deprecate instead
- Move uses direct YAML edit — reliable for well-formed YAML but could fail on unusual formatting
- Deprecate status is qc-atelier-only — qc itself is unaware; deprecated codes can still be applied
- History tab requires server

---

### qc-viz

**Role:** Passive. Visual corpus reader with inline code tags and filtering. No server required at runtime.

**What it does:**

- Renders each interview transcript line by line with inline coloured code tags
- Filtering by category (Johnny Decimal prefix), individual code, speaker, coded/uncoded status
- Whitelist/blacklist system in the Lua filter (e.g. `O_*` open-coding codes excluded by default)
- Export: CSV, JSON, Excel, HTML, PDF — with filter parameters documented in the output
- Filter state persisted to `localStorage` under `qc.viz.state`

**Code schema priority:**

1. `codebook.yaml` (if `codebook.enabled: true` in config)
2. `code_schema.categories` in `qc-atelier-config.yaml`
3. Built-in default schema
4. Auto-generated from first code in each prefix

`codebook.enabled` defaults to `false` — not yet validated against the actual `codebook.yaml` structure.

**Speaker column width:** Computed dynamically as `max(80, min(200, max_speaker_length × 8))` px.

**CDN dependency:** SheetJS and jsPDF loaded unconditionally. CSV/JSON/HTML export work offline; Excel and PDF require network.

**Unimplemented config keys** (defined as stubs, not wired up):

- `display.sections.default_collapsed`
- `display.code_tags.show_full_code`
- Entire `export` block
- Entire `ui` block (including `persistence.storage_key`, hardcoded as `"qc.viz.state"`)

**Key files:**
```
qc-viz.qmd
qc-atelier/qc-viz/
  qc-viz-filter.lua    ← pwd fallback + deep_stringify fix for meta_to_lua
  qc-viz-pre-render.sh ← runs qc codes find --json for each corpus file
  qc-viz.js
  qc-viz.css
```

---

### qc-reflect

**Status: Deprioritised.** LLM-assisted reflexive analysis using Ollama (`qwen3:35b`). Not yet updated to shared CSS tokens or shared Lua path resolution.

---

## Shared infrastructure

### qc-shared.lua

Loaded via `dofile()` at the top of every active filter. Key functions: `read_yaml_file`, `meta_to_lua`, `S` (stringify Pandoc metadata), `N` (numericise), `read_text_file`, `get_json_files`, `read_json_file`, `to_json`, `parse_codebook_yaml`, `flatten_codebook`, `build_use_counts`.

**Shared path resolution pattern** (applied to all three active filters):

```lua
local function _get_project_root_early()
  local s = os.getenv("PANDOC_SCRIPT_FILE") or ""
  local r = s:gsub("/qc-atelier/[^/]+/[^/]+$", "")
  if r ~= "" and r ~= s then return r end
  local h = io.popen("pwd"); local cwd = h:read("*l"); h:close()
  return cwd or "."
end
local _shared_path = _get_project_root_early() .. "/qc-atelier/shared/qc-shared.lua"
local shared = dofile(_shared_path)
```

The `dofile()` approach was chosen over Lua's `require()` because `dofile` takes an absolute path constructable from `PANDOC_SCRIPT_FILE`, avoiding Lua module path configuration in the Pandoc environment.

### qc-shared.css

Shared CSS design tokens. Inlined before every tool's own CSS. Two qc-viz-specific tokens (`--viz-filter-warn-bg`, `--viz-filter-error-bg`) live in `qc-viz.css` rather than the shared file — they have no equivalent in the other tools.

### qc-atelier-server.py

Single Python HTTP server (stdlib only). Started from project root: `python3 qc-atelier-server.py`. Default port 8080. `SERVE_DIR` = `qc/`. `SNAPSHOTS_DIR` = `qc/snapshots/`.

### qc-atelier-config.yaml

Unified config. `QC_ATELIER_CONFIG` env var overrides default path.

### qc/index.html

Static launcher served at `/`. Cards for scheme, viz, refactor (active) and reflect (inactive stub).

---

## Data flow

```
qc (CLI)
  ├── writes → qualitative_coding.sqlite3
  ├── writes → codebook.yaml
  └── reads  ← qc codes rename (from qc-refactor subprocess)

qc-atelier pre-render (qc-viz-pre-render.sh)
  └── qc codes find --json → qc/json/*.json

qc-scheme
  ├── reads  → qc/codebook.json
  └── writes → qc/codebook.json, qc/snapshots/

qc-refactor
  ├── reads  → qc/codebook.json (runtime, inline doc display)
  ├── calls  → qc codes rename → sqlite3 + codebook.yaml
  ├── edits  → codebook.yaml directly (move operations)
  └── writes → qc/codebook.json (provenance, changelog), qc/snapshots/

qc-viz
  └── reads  → qc/json/*.json, qc/corpus/*.txt (render time only)
```

---

## Directory structure

```
project root/
├── qc-atelier-config.yaml
├── qc-atelier-server.py
├── qc-scheme.qmd
├── qc-viz.qmd
├── qc-refactor.qmd
├── qc-reflect.qmd
├── qc-atelier/
│   ├── shared/
│   │   ├── qc-shared.lua
│   │   └── qc-shared.css
│   ├── qc-scheme/
│   ├── qc-viz/
│   ├── qc-refactor/
│   └── qc-reflect/
└── qc/
    ├── codebook.yaml
    ├── codebook.json
    ├── index.html
    ├── qc-scheme.html
    ├── qc-viz.html
    ├── qc-refactor.html
    ├── corpus/
    ├── json/
    └── snapshots/
        ├── lineage.json
        └── codebook_YYYYMMDD-HHMM_label/
            ├── codebook.yaml
            └── codebook.json
```

---

## Server API reference

### Docs

| Endpoint | Description |
|---|---|
| `GET /docs/load?path=X` | Load `codebook.json` |
| `POST /docs/save` | Save `codebook.json` |
| `GET /docs/load-json?path=X` | Load any JSON by absolute path |
| `GET /docs/list-json?dir=X` | List JSON files in a directory |

### Snapshots

| Endpoint | Description |
|---|---|
| `GET /snapshots/list` | List all snapshot dirs with lineage |
| `GET /snapshots/lineage` | Return `lineage.json` |
| `POST /snapshots/create` | Create snapshot or fork |
| `GET /snapshots/read?dir=X` | Read snapshot without changing working state |

### Refactor

| Endpoint | Description |
|---|---|
| `POST /refactor/execute` | Execute staged operations |
| `POST /refactor/move` | Move a code in `codebook.yaml` |
| `GET /refactor/history?path=X` | Return all `refactor` changelog entries, newest first |

### Other

| Endpoint | Description |
|---|---|
| `GET /excerpts/fetch?code=X` | Up to 30 corpus excerpts for a code |
| `POST /logs/save` | Save reflect log |
| `GET /logs/list` | List reflect logs |
| `ANY /api/*` | Proxy to Ollama |

---

## Known limitations and pending work

- Re-render required after qc-refactor execution or after coding in qc
- `qc codes delete` does not exist — use deprecate instead
- qc-reflect not updated to shared standards — deferred
- qc-trace (history/audit tool) — design sketched, not started
- Parent reassignment removed from qc-scheme — now belongs to qc-refactor Move only
- qc-viz codebook tooltip integration deferred
- jsPDF from CDN in qc-scheme and qc-viz — PDF export requires network
- Visual alignment of qc-viz with other tools deferred
- Several qc-viz config keys defined but not wired up: `display.sections.default_collapsed`, `display.code_tags.show_full_code`, `export` block, `ui` block

---

## Decision log

*Sources: `qc-viz-DECISION-LOG.md` (compiled 2026-04-19 from chats `812fff87` and `108d5a39`); `uuuu.qmd` (qc-viz development log, 2026-04-19); compacted transcript of the qc-atelier migration session (chat `108d5a39`); this conversation (2026-04-19).*

*Note on source coverage: the original qc-viz build conversation (`812fff87`, early March 2025) is only partially recoverable — key architectural decisions are documented but turn-by-turn exchanges are not fully accessible. The unification session (`108d5a39`) is well-documented. The qc-refactor implementation work in this conversation is fully documented.*

---

### Early March 2025 — qc-viz initial build (chat `812fff87`)

#### 1.1 — Identifying the need for a corpus reader

**Context.** Working on qc-reflect, the `_quarto.yml` and directory listing of `qc/json/` had just been shared, showing ten per-document coding files (interview dates and respondent initials as filenames). The question of how to pull data into an audit tool prompted a side decision about which JSON format to use.

**Exchange.** Asked whether to build a new format or reuse the existing `qc/json/` exports. Those files were already produced by the pre-render script with `qc codes find --json --pattern "$basename" --before 0 --after 0`. Asked to see the shape of the JSON. Declined to share content directly ("I don't want to paste actual content, it's sensitive data"), so a Python one-liner was provided to print just the key structure. It was run and confirmed the per-document list-of-objects shape with `{code, document, line, text}` fields.

**Decision: reuse `qc/json/*.json` as the data source for any corpus-facing tool.**
Rationale: the files already existed, were up to date, and contained the `text` field with full coded segment content. No new export format needed.

#### 1.2 — Separating corpus visualisation from the audit tool

**Decision: build qc-viz as a separate tool from qc-reflect, not as a panel inside it.**
The initial framing was qc-reflect as a unified audit interface, but line-by-line corpus reading (what qc-viz does) is a different concern from LLM-assisted code analysis (what qc-reflect does). Agreed to keep them separate, following the `qc` philosophy of single-purpose tools.

#### 1.3 — Architecture: static HTML, no server

**Decision: qc-viz would be purely static — all data baked into HTML at render time, no runtime server calls.**
This was a deliberate contrast to qc-scheme (which requires a server for read/write of `codebook.docs.json`). The rationale: qc-viz is read-only. It displays what has already been coded; it writes nothing back. A server dependency would add startup friction for what is essentially a document viewer. This made qc-viz the only tool in the ecosystem with no server component.

**Implication accepted:** any change to corpus files or coding requires a re-render. Confirmed acceptable given that re-render is already the workflow trigger for qc-viz.

#### 1.4 — Pre-render script and data pipeline

**Decision: use a bash pre-render script (`qc-viz-pre-render.sh`) to generate `qc/json/*.json` before the Lua filter runs.**
The script activates the Python venv, iterates over `qc/corpus/*.txt`, checks for files in `qc/corpus/exclude/` to skip, and calls `qc codes find --json` for each.

**Bug introduced.** At some point during initial development the bash script was accidentally duplicated — a second `#!/bin/bash` shebang and a copy of the inner loop were appended. The second copy lacked the config-reading logic present in the first half. Not caught during initial development; remained until Step 3 of the unification work (2026-03-25).

#### 1.5 — Config file: `qc-viz-config.yaml`

**Decision: qc-viz gets its own config file, separate from `qc-reflect-config.yaml`.**
At the time, the ecosystem config was named `qc-reflect-config.yaml` and was understood as scoped to qc-reflect and qc-scheme. qc-viz was being developed independently. The config was given a `qc-viz-config.yaml` file with its own `directories`, `files`, `code_filters`, `code_schema`, `display`, `codebook`, `export`, `ui`, and `advanced` sections.

**Known issue introduced.** This created duplication: `corpus_dir`, `json_dir`, and `exclude_dir` were defined in both config files. Flagged as a known divergence; marked for resolution in the unification work.

#### 1.6 — Code filtering system

**Decision: implement a whitelist/blacklist filtering system in the Lua filter to control which codes appear in the visualisation.**
Motivation: deprecated or open-coding codes in the codebook should not appear in a clean visualisation. The blacklist was populated with a list of `O_*`-prefixed open-coding codes.

The filter logic operates on four matching mechanisms: exact code string, two-digit prefix, prefix range, and Lua regex pattern. Whitelist mode (if enabled) takes precedence over blacklist mode.

#### 1.7 — Johnny Decimal prefix system for category display

**Decision: use the first two digits of codes (the Johnny Decimal prefix) as the grouping unit for the filter panel.**
Each prefix gets a colour from a 20-colour rotating palette, and a label derived from: codebook (if integration enabled) → config `categories` map → built-in default schema → auto-generated from first code in prefix.

**The `codebook.enabled` flag.** Codebook integration was implemented but defaulted to `false`. Rationale: the codebook YAML parser was written but the integration was not yet validated against the actual `codebook.yaml` structure. Enabling by default risked silently wrong category labels.

#### 1.8 — UI design

**Decision: full-width filter panel at top, one collapsible section per interview below.**
Each section contains a four-column table (line number, speaker, text, codes) with all lines rendered — both coded and uncoded. Uncoded lines are hidden by default (toggle in filter panel). Gap rows show counts of hidden lines between visible ones.

**Speaker column width.** Computed dynamically: max speaker name length × 8 pixels, clamped to [80, 200]. Chosen to avoid column layout breaking on long participant identifiers without hardcoding a fixed width.

**Stats bar.** Each interview section has a summary row below the table showing visible/hidden counts, broken down by coded vs uncoded. Updated on every filter change via `updateStats()`.

#### 1.9 — Export system

**Decision: implement CSV, JSON, Excel, HTML, and PDF exports client-side, with filter parameters documented in the output.**
A researcher filtering to specific codes and speakers should be able to export exactly what they see, with a record of what filter was applied. The filter parameter documentation was treated as a methodological transparency feature.

**CDN dependency accepted.** SheetJS and jsPDF are loaded from `cdnjs.cloudflare.com` unconditionally at page load. CSV, JSON, and HTML exports have no library dependency and work offline. Excel and PDF require the CDN. Accepted on the grounds that the research environment has internet access.

#### 1.10 — localStorage persistence

**Decision: persist all filter state to `localStorage` under the key `"qc-viz-state"`.**
Rationale: the filter panel has enough state (per-code checkboxes, per-speaker checkboxes, uncoded toggle, section collapse states) that losing it on every reload would be frustrating. Save is synchronous (no debounce) because the state object is small.

**Key not namespaced.** The key `"qc-viz-state"` was chosen without a tool prefix. Flagged as a future migration task.

#### 1.11 — Unimplemented config keys

Several config keys were defined in `qc-viz-config.yaml` during design but never wired up in the Lua filter or JS:

- `display.sections.default_collapsed` — categories always start collapsed regardless
- `display.code_tags.show_full_code` — full code always shown regardless
- The entire `export` block — timestamp format, filename prefix
- The entire `ui` block — includes `persistence.storage_key`, which was hardcoded as `"qc-viz-state"` in JS instead of being read from config

**Decision: leave these as stubs.**
Rationale: the config keys were included during design to make the file self-documenting (showing what could be configured), but implementing them all before first use was over-engineering. Flagged in the config file with comments.

---

### 2026-04-19 — qc-viz interface simplification

*Source: `uuuu.qmd`. Session conducted ~14:00–16:50 UTC.*

#### Context

The researcher was seeing the old multi-mode GHI (Grey/Hide/Invisible) interface despite having already updated individual code items to simplified single-checkbox format. Previous attempts to fix it (updating Lua and JS files, clearing browser cache, hard refresh) had not resolved it.

#### 14:12 — File set received

Six files provided: `qc-viz-config.yaml` (166 lines), `qc-viz-filter.lua` (2447 lines), `qc-viz-pre-render.sh` (122 lines), `qc-viz.css` (333 lines), `qc-viz.js` (576 lines), `README.md` (429 lines).

#### 14:15–14:25 — Root cause analysis

**Finding 1: Duplicate filter header (lines 667–747 in `qc-viz-filter.lua`).**
Two `<div class="filter-header">` blocks existed, the second nested inside the first with a `style="margin-top: 0.5rem; padding-top: 0.5rem; border-top: 1px solid #dee2e6;"` attribute. This produced confusing HTML structure with duplicate controls.

**Finding 2: Old GHI mode buttons (lines 763–770).**
```lua
html = html .. '          <div class="category-controls-compact">\n'
html = html .. '            <button class="mode-all-btn" data-prefix="' .. prefix .. '" data-mode="grey" title="Check all Grey">G</button>\n'
html = html .. '            <button class="mode-all-btn" data-prefix="' .. prefix .. '" data-mode="hide" title="Check all Hide">H</button>\n'
html = html .. '            <button class="mode-all-btn" data-prefix="' .. prefix .. '" data-mode="inverse" title="Toggle all Inverse">I</button>\n'
html = html .. '          </div>\n'
```
Old multi-mode interface still present in category headers.

**Finding 3: Old codes-header column labels (lines 773–780).**
```lua
html = html .. '          <div class="codes-header">\n'
html = html .. '            <span class="code-name-col">Code</span>\n'
html = html .. '            <span class="mode-col" title="Grey: Dim/fade out this code">G</span>\n'
html = html .. '            <span class="mode-col" title="Hide: Hide rows containing this code">H</span>\n'
html = html .. '            <span class="mode-col" title="Inverse: Invert the selection logic">I</span>\n'
html = html .. '          </div>\n'
```

**Finding 4: Individual code items already correct.**
The `<input type="checkbox">` elements had already been updated to the simplified structure by the researcher. The problem was the surrounding scaffolding.

#### 14:28–14:30 — Filter header and category header fixes

- Consolidated duplicate filter headers into a single `<div class="filter-header">` with one set of controls
- Replaced GHI mode buttons with a single `<button class="select-all-btn" data-prefix="...">All</button>` per category
- Removed codes-header column label row entirely

#### 15:15 — Scope expanded: release preparation

**User request:**
> "Select/de-select all seem to do nothing. Remove them if that's the case. Also, shift all styling to the css file, if you deem it safe and appropriate. And re-write the readme. I want to make this available for release as a standalone git repo using the instructions provided at [Medium article]. Make this as easy as possible."

#### 15:18 — Investigation: non-functional buttons

Found at lines 707–708:
```lua
html = html .. '        <button class="action-btn" id="select-all-codes">Select All</button>\n'
html = html .. '        <button class="action-btn" id="deselect-all-codes">Deselect All</button>\n'
```
Searched `qc-viz.js` for corresponding event handlers. Handlers existed for `clear-all-filters`, `speaker-select-all`, `speaker-select-none` but not for `select-all-codes` or `deselect-all-codes`. Buttons were orphaned HTML with no JS wired up.

**Decision: remove both buttons.**

#### 15:20 — Investigation: inline styles

Found two instances:

**Instance 1** (line ~675): `<div style="position: relative;">` wrapping the export menu. Straightforward to move to CSS.

**Instance 2** (line ~758): `<div class="filter-category" data-prefix="' .. prefix .. '" style="border-left-color: ' .. color .. ';">` — dynamic colour set by Lua from `get_prefix_color(prefix, code_colors)`. Cannot hardcode in CSS.

**Decision: apply border colours via JavaScript.**
Three options considered: (a) keep inline style — not ideal, (b) generate CSS dynamically in Lua — complex, (c) apply via JS after page load — best practice for separation of concerns. Lua generates code tags with `style="background-color: #COLOR"`. JS reads the background colour from the first code tag of each prefix and applies it to the category border. This maintains colour consistency without duplicating colour logic.

#### 15:25–15:33 — Implementation

- Removed non-functional `select-all-codes` and `deselect-all-codes` buttons (lines 707–708)
- Replaced `style="position: relative;"` with class `export-dropdown` (line ~675)
- Removed `style="border-left-color: ' .. color .. ';"` from category div (line ~758)
- Added `.export-dropdown { position: relative; }` to `qc-viz.css`
- Added `initializeFilters()` code in `qc-viz.js`:
```javascript
document.querySelectorAll('.filter-category').forEach(category => {
  const prefix = category.dataset.prefix;
  const firstCodeTag = document.querySelector(`.code-tag[data-prefix="${prefix}"]`);
  if (firstCodeTag) {
    const color = firstCodeTag.style.backgroundColor;
    category.style.borderLeftColor = color;
  }
});
```

#### 15:40–15:55 — README rewrite and release guide

New README written for standalone release (~500 lines). Two git extraction methods documented in `RELEASE.md` (~400 lines): git filter-branch (preserves history) and git archive (clean slate). README updated to discourage editing `default_code_schema` directly in `qc-viz-filter.lua`, pointing to config file instead.

#### 16:35 — Code schema documentation added to README

**Investigation of existing code:**
- `parse_codebook()` at lines 235–263 in `qc-viz-filter.lua`
- `load_code_schema_from_codebook()` at lines 266–305

Schema priority order:
1. Codebook (if `codebook.enabled = true`)
2. Config file (`code_schema.categories`)
3. Built-in defaults
4. Auto-generated from first code in category

Section added to README covering: how codebook parsing works, schema priority, enabling/disabling, custom schema configuration, colour assignment, troubleshooting.

**Net change from this session:** `qc-viz-filter.lua` reduced from 2447 to ~880 lines.

---

### 2026-03-25 — Unification session begins (chat `108d5a39`)

*Source: compacted transcript of chat `108d5a39`.*

**Starting point.** Three separate tools (qc-scheme, qc-reflect, qc-viz), each with its own config, with qc-reflect's server shared informally with qc-scheme. Documentation had been produced for all three tools. The goal: unify under shared server, shared config, shared CSS, shared Lua helpers, shared navigation, and shared localStorage convention.

Before work began, the sidecar file rename was verified complete: `codebook.docs.json` had already been renamed to `codebook.json`, and no executable code retained the old name.

#### Naming decision — qc-atelier

During Step 1, the researcher proposed differentiating the suite from the main `qc` tool since they are not the primary qc developer. Name directions proposed:

- Derivation/layering: `qc-atelier`, `qc-studio`, `qc-lab`
- Metaphor: `qc-forge`, `qc-loom`
- Function names

**Decision: qc-atelier** — a workshop or studio space where skilled work happens on top of raw materials. The `qc-` prefix was explicitly retained to maintain the connection to the underlying tool.

#### Step 1 — Rename and ecosystem naming

Actions:
- `git mv qc-reflect-server.py qc-atelier-server.py`
- `git mv qc-reflect-config.yaml qc-atelier-config.yaml`
- Env var: `QC_REFLECT_CONFIG` → `QC_ATELIER_CONFIG` (all filter files)
- Env var: `QC_VIZ_CONFIG` → `QC_ATELIER_CONFIG` (qc-viz filter)
- Server log prefix: `[qc-server]` → `[qc-atelier-server]`
- Config file header updated to `# qc-atelier Configuration File`
- All references updated in: `qc-reflect.js`, `qc-scheme-filter.lua`, `qc-viz-filter.lua`, `qc-reflect-filter.lua`

Commit: `feat: rename to qc-atelier — unified server, config, and ecosystem name`

#### Step 2 — Config unification

`qc-viz-config.yaml` merged into `qc-atelier-config.yaml` under a `viz:` section. The `load_config()` function in `qc-viz-filter.lua` updated: reads shared top-level `directories` and `code_schema` first via `merge(config.directories, yaml_lua.directories)` and `merge(config.code_schema, yaml_lua.code_schema)`, then overlays keys from the `viz:` section (`viz.files`, `viz.code_filters`, `viz.display`, `viz.codebook`, `viz.advanced`). The filter works even if some keys are omitted from `viz:` — falls back to shared directory paths.

The pre-render bash script required no changes — its grep patterns target two-space-indented keys under `directories:`, which remained at the same nesting level. `qc-viz-config.yaml` deleted.

Commit: `feat: merge qc-viz-config.yaml into qc-atelier-config.yaml`

#### Step 3 — Pre-render script fixes

**Bug 1: dead code loop.** The script contained a second `#!/bin/bash` shebang at approximately line 98, followed by a complete copy of the inner loop without config-reading logic. Everything from `echo "JSON generation complete"\n#!/bin/bash` to EOF was dead — the config-less original version accidentally left in place. Fix: delete from the second shebang to EOF.

**Bug 2: `json_line_offset` not applied.** The filter had `lua_line_num = entry.line + 1` hardcoded at line 877. `config.advanced.json_line_offset` was parsed but never read. Fix: `+ (config.advanced.json_line_offset or 1)`. Rationale: the offset exists because `qc codes find --json` produces 0-indexed line numbers while the corpus display is 1-indexed; honouring the config key allows adjustment if qc's behaviour changes.

Commit: `fix: remove dead code from pre-render script; apply json_line_offset`

#### Step 4 — Shared Lua helpers

`qc-shared.lua` extracted from duplicated code in both filters. Functions shared: `read_yaml_file`, `meta_to_lua`, `read_text_file`, `get_json_files`, `read_json_file`, `to_json`, `parse_codebook_yaml`, `flatten_codebook`, `build_use_counts`, `S`, `N`. Loaded via `dofile()` rather than `require()` because `dofile` takes an absolute path constructable from `PANDOC_SCRIPT_FILE`.

Also fixed: dead `parse_tree` function removed from qc-viz-filter. Stale `qc-codebook-docs` path pattern removed from qc-scheme-filter. qc-reflect excluded — tool deprioritised.

Commit: `feat: extract shared Lua helpers into qc-shared.lua`

#### Step 5 — Shared CSS variables

Before writing, a Python mapping compared qc-viz's hardcoded hex values against qc-scheme's token set. Almost every value had a direct equivalent. Two did not:
- `#fff3cd` — yellow-tinted background for partial code selection in filter panel
- `#f8d7da` — red-tinted background for empty selection in filter panel

These became qc-viz-local tokens (`--viz-filter-warn-bg`, `--viz-filter-error-bg`) rather than being promoted to the shared palette since no other tool needs them.

Both filters updated to inline `qc-shared.css` before tool-specific CSS via two `read_text_file()` calls concatenated into a single `<style>` block. qc-viz gained IBM Plex Sans/Mono font references via the token system, bringing its typography into alignment.

Commit: `feat: extract shared CSS design tokens into qc-shared.css`

#### Step 6 — localStorage namespace migration

- qc-scheme.js: `qccd.*` → `qc.scheme.*`. One-time migration shim on first load copies existing state and deletes old keys.
- qc-viz.js: `"qc-viz-state"` → `"qc.viz.state"`. Migration shim added.

Commit: `feat: migrate localStorage namespaces to qc.scheme.* and qc.viz.*`

#### Step 7 — Shared navigation bar

**URL convention decision.** Asked whether to use server-relative or file-relative URLs. Server-relative (`/qc-scheme.html`, `/qc-viz.html`) chosen because: (a) both tools are always served via `qc-atelier-server.py`, (b) file-relative URLs break when opening directly as `file://` regardless, (c) server-relative URLs are simpler. Agreed.

Nav injected by each Lua filter as a static `<nav>` HTML string immediately after `<body>`. Nav CSS appended to `qc-shared.css`. Nav also added to launcher `index.html`.

Commit: `feat: add shared navigation bar to qc-scheme and qc-viz`

#### Step 8 — Launcher

`qc/index.html` added, served at `/`. Cards for scheme, viz (active), reflect (inactive stub with visual indication). Server startup message updated to show launcher URL. Originally deferred until qc-reflect was active — decision made to add anyway since launcher value is immediate even with three tools.

Commit: `feat: add qc-atelier launcher at / with tool cards`

---

### 2026-03 — Post-migration work

#### Repository structure and .gitignore

**Repository structure clarified.** `CITF-Postdoc/` is the main public GitHub repo. `qc/`, `private/`, `data/` are git submodules on a private university GitLab instance. `qc/corpus/*.txt` files are the definitive interview transcripts — more refined than the `data/` versions — and must be preserved. `qc/json/` files are always reproducible via the pre-render script.

**Existing `qc/.gitignore` at this point:**
```
qc-viz.html
qc-scheme.html
qc-reflect.html
*_files/
json/
*.log
bin/ lib/ include/ share/ pyvenv.cfg
.DS_Store  [etc.]
```

**Problem.** Test noise had accumulated in three places not covered by the existing ignore: `snapshots/` (test snapshots from development), `codebook.json` (modified during testing), `.working_parent` (ephemeral server state).

**Exchange detail.** An initial `.gitignore` was written that omitted some entries from the existing file. Researcher noticed: "Where did all the other stuff go?" Asked to paste the full file. Researcher clarified they were thinking of the top-level template gitignore, not the `qc/`-specific one. Top-level template shared; entries merged, excluding project-root-specific ones (`.luarc.json`, `_site/`, `_extensions/`, `_freeze/`).

**Three entries added:**

- `.working_parent` — ephemeral server state, meaningless at rest
- `snapshots/` — ignored by default; add selectively with `git add -f` at analytical milestones. Rationale: development had produced test snapshots not representing meaningful analytical checkpoints; ignoring by default prevents accidental blanket commits.
- `codebook.json` — untracked during development; begin tracking when real research is underway, committing alongside named snapshots.

Commit:
```
chore: add .gitignore to qc submodule

Formalises what was previously implicit — establishes which files are
intentionally untracked in the private GitLab remote.

Carries over the standard template (compiled binaries, packages, OS
files, R, LaTeX, Python venv) and adds qc-atelier-specific entries:

- .working_parent — ephemeral server state, meaningless at rest
- snapshots/       — ignored by default; add selectively at analytical
                     milestones with git add -f
- codebook.json    — untracked during development; begin tracking when
                     real research is underway
```

#### Visual alignment deferred

Asked to update qc-viz to be visually aligned with the other utilities. After discussion of what "alignment" meant (token alignment vs structural similarity), researcher said: "Actually, nevermind. There is more to think about, still." No code changes made. The shared CSS tokens from Step 5 provide a baseline.

---

### 2026-04-12 — qc-scheme overhaul proposed → qc-refactor born

**Researcher request (2026-04-12T08:41:27Z):**

> "I want to do a bit of an overhaul to qc-scheme. I want this to be a tool for re-shaping the code system and tracking those changes, in addition to documenting the codes themselves."

The researcher envisioned qc-scheme becoming the tool for both documentation and structural operations (rename, merge, move, deprecate).

**Design response and key reframing.**
Rather than adding structural operations to qc-scheme, the proposal was to spin them off into a new tool. Rationale:

1. qc-scheme's identity is passive/responsive — it documents and presents. Adding execution machinery would blur this.
2. Structural operations are fundamentally different in weight and consequence from documentation edits — they affect the database and require dry-run preview, scripting, and versioning.
3. A dedicated tool can be used by other tools (qc-reflect in future) to populate an operation queue from LLM suggestions.

**Decision: spin off as qc-refactor.**
Names considered: `qc-reshape`, `qc-revise`, `qc-refine`, `qc-forge`, `qc-draft`, `qc-compose`. Researcher chose `qc-refactor` — the programming connotation (changing structure without changing external meaning) is appropriate for a researcher with computational background.

*Detailed qc-refactor implementation happened in this conversation (2026-04-19) after the compacted transcript boundary.*

---

### 2026-04-19 — qc-refactor implementation

#### Investigation of qc source code

`corpus.py` and `models.py` read directly from the installed package. Key findings:

- `qualitative_coding.sqlite3` is the live source of truth (SQLAlchemy/SQLite)
- `qc codes rename` updates `CodedLine.code_id` then calls `update_codebook()` — `codebook.yaml` is derived, not primary
- `update_codebook()` adds missing codes to YAML but does not remove unused ones
- No `qc codes delete` CLI command exists
- No `qc codes move` CLI command exists
- `qc/json/*.json` files are qc-atelier render-time exports, not qc's own data

**Decision: no direct database access.**
qc-atelier interacts with qc's data only through CLI commands. Direct SQLite writes bypass SQLAlchemy's session management and risk database/YAML sync corruption.

**Decision: delete deferred, deprecate used instead.**
No CLI command for deletion. Two alternatives considered: (a) create a `_deprecated` branch in `codebook.yaml` visible to qc itself, or (b) mark status in `codebook.json` only. Chose (b) — enforcement is unnecessary because code application is manual and visible through VSCode autocomplete.

**Decision: move via direct `codebook.yaml` edit.**
No `qc codes move` CLI command. Safe because renames go database → YAML (not the reverse), so direct YAML hierarchy edits don't conflict with qc's sync model. Server `_refactor_move` handler edits by regex line-matching — finds the code, extracts it and any children, reindents, reinserts under new parent.

**Decision: CORPUS_DATA baked at render time.**
Full `{document, line, text}` entries per code baked as `CORPUS_DATA` global, capped at 30/code. Enables corpus impact preview without server calls. Consistent with qc-viz's static-first approach.

**Decision: skeleton HTML in Lua filter, JS at end of body.**
qc-refactor originally built skeleton HTML as JS string arrays in `DOMContentLoaded`. This caused a `SyntaxError` from an unescaped apostrophe in `"you're"` inside a single-quoted string literal. The architectural fix: move skeleton to Lua filter as a `[[...]]` string block. JS script tag placed after skeleton HTML, before `</body>`. `defer` not used — only works on external scripts with `src=`, not inline scripts.

**Decision: QMD filter declaration must be under `format: html: filters:`.**
Top-level `filters:` placement causes `PANDOC_SCRIPT_FILE` not to be set by Pandoc, breaking shared path resolution. Applies to all qc-atelier QMDs.

**Decision: separate queues per operation type.**
Initially unified queue. Separated into per-type queues (rename/merge/move/deprecate) so the active form and its queue are co-located. Each tab shows its own queue list below the form.

**Decision: swap control in merge form, not queue.**
Swap button (promote first source to target, demote current target to first source) belongs in the merge form. The queue is a read-only record of staged operations — not an editing surface.

**Decision: session note replaces execution modal.**
Originally a summary modal appeared on execute. Replaced with a persistent session note textarea in the queue panel, always visible. Execute fires directly without popup. Motivation: the modal framed the note as a gate rather than a working surface for analytical thinking. The note should be written as decisions are made, not as a final formality under pressure of committing.

**Decision: session note is the primary versioning mechanism.**
The note becomes the snapshot label, seeds the `provenance` field of affected codes, and is stored in the changelog. Execute button disabled until operations are staged and a note is written.

**Decision: merge documentation — sources read-only, target editable.**
When merging, source codes show a read-only summary of their existing documentation (they are disappearing). Target panel is editable and pre-populated from source notes with attribution `[from SourceCode]: ...`. Rationale: it makes no sense to edit documentation for codes about to be removed; their notes should inform the target's documentation.

**Decision: parent reassignment removed from qc-scheme.**
The Parent dropdown in qc-scheme's Documentation tab and the bulk toolbar Parent selector were removed. Structural moves now belong exclusively to qc-refactor Move. The underlying `treeOverrides` state and `reparent`/`wouldCycle` functions were retained in qc-scheme.js for the rendering index machinery.

Commit: `cleanup: remove parent reassignment UI from qc-scheme; fix shared path resolution`

**Decision: documentation fields kept separate.**
Five fields (`scope`, `rationale`, `usage_notes`, `provenance`, `examples`) debated for consolidation. `scope`+`usage_notes` are both definitional (positive vs negative). `rationale`+`provenance` are both "why" (analytical vs historical). Decision: keep all five separate. Each answers a subtly different question.

`provenance` specifically must remain distinct from `rationale`: it is the field qc-refactor writes to automatically on execution. Machine-written genealogical notes must not overwrite researcher-written analytical reasoning.

---

### 2026-04-19 — Rendering and filter fixes

**Shared path resolution bug fixed.**
The bare gsub pattern `_script:gsub("/qc-atelier/[^/]+/[^/]+$", "")` fails when `PANDOC_SCRIPT_FILE` is unset, producing a path starting with `/assets/...` with no project prefix. Fixed with `_get_project_root_early()` and `pwd` fallback. Applied to:

- `qc-scheme-filter.lua` — fixed; second pass required when a remaining `_script` reference was found at the `SHARED_CSS_FILE` assignment line
- `qc-refactor-filter.lua` — fixed from the start
- `qc-viz-filter.lua` — fixed; two `_script` references found and fixed: one for the shared Lua module path, one for `SHARED_CSS_FILE`

Commits:
```
fix: apply pwd fallback to qc-scheme-filter shared path resolution
fix: apply pwd fallback to qc-viz-filter shared path resolution
fix: move JS script tag to end of body in qc-refactor filter
```

**qc-viz `meta_to_lua` coercion bug — pre-existing, surfaced by path fix.**
After the path fix, `qc-viz-filter.lua` rendered further and hit a pre-existing bug: `meta_to_lua` leaves nested Pandoc metadata objects unconverted when config YAML values are deeply nested. `config.files.output_file`, `config.directories.json_dir`, etc. arrived as Pandoc table objects, causing concatenation failures.

This was iterative — each render exposed a new failure at a different line (`output_path` concatenation, then `json_dir`, then `corpus_dir`, then `CSS_FILE`, then `JS_FILE`). The systemic fix: `deep_stringify` pass over `yaml_lua` immediately after `meta_to_lua` conversion, before any merging into config. Uses `pcall(pandoc.utils.stringify, v)` on each value. `pandoc.utils.stringify` also applied at all module-level path variable assignments as an additional safeguard.

The bug was pre-existing — qc-viz had previously been rendering because `PANDOC_SCRIPT_FILE` was set in that context (causing the old path code to succeed), so the filter never reached the config-merge code path. The path fix unblocked rendering far enough to expose the underlying issue.

Commit:
```
fix: qc-viz-filter shared path resolution and meta_to_lua string coercion

Apply _get_project_root_early() pwd fallback for shared module path,
matching the fix already applied to qc-scheme-filter and
qc-refactor-filter.

Fix pre-existing bug where meta_to_lua left nested Pandoc metadata
objects unconverted after YAML config loading. Added deep_stringify
pass over yaml_lua before merging into config. Applied
pandoc.utils.stringify at module-level path variable assignments
as an additional safeguard.
```

---

### 2026-04-21 — Directory restructure: qc-atelier/ → qc-atelier/

All tool scripts moved from `qc-atelier/{tool}/` to `qc-atelier/{tool}/`.
Shared scripts moved to `qc-atelier/shared/`. Pre-render script moved from
`qc-viz/qc-viz-pre-render.sh` to `qc-atelier/shared/qc-pre-render.sh` — it
was never qc-viz-specific and should be shared. Documentation moved to
`qc-atelier/docs/`. All path references updated in filters, QMDs, server,
and config.

### 2026-04-20 — Export panel fix and nav brand link

**Export pill button added to qc-scheme sidebar.**
The export panel previously had a full-width button in a separate action row. Replaced with an `ep-chip ep-export-btn` pill appended directly to the format chips row in `buildExportPanel()`. Styled with accent background and hover state.

**Export panel visibility fixed.**
The export panel was being clipped by `.split { overflow: hidden }` because the sidebar height was not bounded. The flex height chain (`#qc-scheme-root` → `.app` → `.split` → `.sidebar`) was not propagating a definite height. Fixed by adding `max-height: calc(100vh - 110px)` to `.sidebar`, `min-height: 0` to `.split` and `.tree`, and removing `overflow: hidden` from `.sidebar`.

**Nav brand converted from `<span>` to `<a href="/">`.**
All three active filters (qc-scheme, qc-refactor, qc-viz) had the qc-atelier brand as a non-linked `<span>`. Changed to `<a href="/">` so the brand links back to the launcher.

**qc-viz config stubs restored.**
`sections` and `code_tags` sub-tables were missing from the display config defaults in `qc-viz-filter.lua`, causing a nil index error. Restored with comments noting they are defined but not yet wired up.

**`wouldCycle` and `reparent` restored in qc-scheme.js.**
These functions had been accidentally removed during dead code cleanup. They are live — used by the rendering index machinery (treeOverrides). Restored.

### Deferred / planned

**qc-trace** — proposed unified history/audit tool. Cross-tool timeline interleaving qc-scheme edits, qc-refactor sessions, and qc CLI operations (from `qualitative_coding.log`), snapshot lineage graph, codebook state at any historical point. Name chosen: `qc-trace`. Design sketched, implementation not started.

**qc-reflect reintegration** — CSS tokens and Lua path resolution not yet applied. Deferred until the tool is actively used analytically.

**qc-viz codebook tooltip integration** — would require baking `codebook.json` at render time or adding a server dependency. Deferred.

**Re-render prompting after qc-refactor execution** — no prompt or mechanism currently. Manual `quarto render` required. Deferred.

**Field consolidation in qc-scheme** — `scope`+`usage_notes` and `rationale`+`provenance` discussed as candidates for merging. Decision: keep separate for now. Visual grouping in UI planned but not implemented.

**qc-viz visual alignment** — shared CSS tokens provide a baseline. Full UI structural alignment deferred as an open design question.

**Code quality sweep** — take stock of the full codebase: identify anything that is unclear, redundant, inconsistent, inefficient, or fragile across all four tools and shared infrastructure. Produce a docket of specific actions to resolve problems, streamline the system, and bring everything to a professional standard.

### 2026-04-22 — qc-pre-render.sh YAML parsing fix

`qc-pre-render.sh` was printing raw YAML lines (including inline comments) as config values. The grep/sed chain in `read_yaml_config()` captured `qc/corpus   # Interview transcripts` rather than `qc/corpus`, causing the corpus directory check to fail on render.

Fixed by replacing per-key sed pipelines with a `_parse_yaml_value()` helper that strips inline comments (`# ...`), trailing whitespace, and quotes in order. All five config keys use the helper.

---

### 2026-04-22 — qc-refactor overhaul

**Script generation.** `generateScript()` previously emitted commented pseudo-instructions for move and deprecate. Now returns only executable `qc codes rename` calls. When the queue contains only server-side ops (move, deprecate), the Script panel renders the `#!/bin/bash` header with a prominent explanatory note. The note is dynamically shown below the script block when server-side ops are mixed with CLI ops.

**Corpus impact.** Removed erroneous filter that excluded move ops from the impact panel. All four operation types now show corpus segments for affected codes. `affectedCodes` logic updated: rename/merge show sources plus existing target; move/deprecate show sources only.

**Field descriptions.** `codePanelHTML` updated to include hints (subtitle on label) and placeholder text for all four documentation fields, matching qc-scheme exactly.

**Queue editing.** Edit button (✎) added per queue item. Clicking removes the item from the queue, switches to the appropriate op tab, and calls `repopulateForm()` to restore values into the form.

**Unified queue.** Per-type queues (`state.queues.rename` etc.) replaced with a single `state.queue` array. Single `#queue-list` element in the HTML skeleton. All op types appear together with type badges.

**Inline validation errors.** All `alert()` calls replaced with `showFormError()`, which renders a red inline message in the form that auto-dismisses after 3 seconds. Execution errors shown in the Results panel.

**Missing move badge.** `badge-move` CSS rule was absent, causing transparent labels. Added with amber colouring consistent with the existing palette.

---

### 2026-04-22 — Server refactor routes; snapshot/execute decoupling

**Missing server routes.** `/refactor/execute`, `/refactor/move`, and `/refactor/history` were absent from `qc-atelier-server.py` — the file in use was a pre-qc-refactor version. All three handlers added:

- `_refactor_execute` — runs rename/merge ops via `qc codes rename` subprocess, move ops via `_do_move`, deprecate ops via direct `codebook.json` edit. Updates provenance fields and appends a `refactor` changelog entry. No snapshot created.
- `_refactor_move` — thin wrapper around `_do_move` for standalone move calls.
- `_do_move` — direct line-level manipulation of `codebook.yaml`: finds the code block, extracts it with children, re-indents, reinserts under new parent or at top level.
- `_refactor_history` — reads `refactor`-type entries from `codebook.json` changelog, returns newest first.

**Snapshot/execute decoupling.** Auto-snapshot on execute removed. Snapshots are now created only through a manual action (to be implemented). The session note is a commit-style description written into the changelog `summary` field and used to seed code provenance — it is no longer used as a snapshot label and the snapshot name preview has been removed from the UI.

**Server log filter** extended to include `/refactor/` and `/snapshots/` routes.

---

### 2026-04-22 — Rename/merge execution fixes; orphan cleanup; tree refresh

**Subprocess `cwd` fix.** `qc codes rename` requires `settings.yaml` in the working directory. `cwd` was set to `SERVE_DIR.parent` (project root) but `settings.yaml` lives in `SERVE_DIR` (`qc/`). Fixed to `cwd=SERVE_DIR`.

**`qc_bin` resolution.** Added `qc_bin` config key under `server:` in `qc-atelier-config.yaml`. Server resolves via `CONFIG.get("qc_bin") or shutil.which("qc") or "qc"`. Required because subprocess PATH does not include pipx bin directory. numpy 1.26.4 → 2.4.4 upgrade in pipx venv was also required to fix broken compiled extensions against Python 3.14.3.

**`_docs_load` URL-decode fix.** The `path` query parameter was not being URL-decoded, so absolute paths with encoded slashes were passed directly to `Path()`, silently returning empty codes. Fixed with `urllib.parse.unquote()`.

**Config env var fix.** Server was reading `QC_REFLECT_CONFIG` / `qc-reflect-config.yaml` — updated to `QC_ATELIER_CONFIG` / `qc-atelier-config.yaml`.

**Orphan cleanup after rename/merge.** `qc codes rename` updates SQLite and calls `update_codebook()` which adds the new name to `codebook.yaml` but does not remove the old name. After a successful rename/merge, server now runs `qc codes stats -zu 0` to identify zero-use codes, intersects with the operation's source codes, and strips confirmed orphans from `codebook.yaml` via `_do_remove`. Sources that still have corpus applications are skipped. This follows upstream issue #94 — `qc codebook --clean` is the planned CLI solution; `_do_remove` is the local workaround until it lands.

**`/refactor/tree` endpoint.** New `GET /refactor/tree` handler parses `codebook.yaml` into a flat `{name, parent, depth, prefix}` list. Called by JS `refreshTree()` on page load and after every execute, updating `_runtimeTree` so pickers reflect changes without re-render.

**History panel failure transparency.** Changelog entry now includes a `status` field (`ok`/`partial`/`failed`). Provenance is only written for successful ops. History panel shows per-op ✓/✗ icons with error output inline for failed ops.

### 2026-04-22 — Bulk move; queue panel layout fix

**Bulk move.** Move tab now renders a multi-select checklist instead of a single code picker. All codes in the tree are listed with indentation reflecting hierarchy. Branch collapse toggles (▶/▼) persist collapse state in `state.pickerCollapsed` across opens. Checking a code adds it to `state.moveSelected`; the doc panel updates to the most recently checked code. "Add to queue" generates one move op per selected code. Selection persists visually after queuing and is cleared only on execute or clear-all.

**Queue panel layout fix.** The left panel had large empty space below the footer and the queue list had no scroll. Root cause: `#qc-refactor-root` had no CSS, so it didn't participate in the flex height chain — `body` (100vh flex column) → `#qc-refactor-root` (unsized) → `.app` (flex:1) → `.queue-panel`. The missing link meant `.app` couldn't stretch to fill the viewport. Fixed by giving `#qc-refactor-root` `display:flex; flex-direction:column; flex:1; min-height:0`. Queue list now has `flex:1` and scrolls; form is capped; session note and footer are pinned.

**Note for qc-scheme.** The sidebar height workaround (currently `max-height: calc(100vh - 110px)`) is likely the same root cause — `#qc-scheme-root` or an intermediate container missing from the flex chain. Resolve by auditing the height chain in qc-scheme the same way.
